name: Embalse de José Toran a Mirador de las Palomas - circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Puebla De Los Infantes offers a diverse landscape in the northeastern sector of Seville, Spain. The region transitions from the fertile plains of the Guadalquivir Valley to the foothills of the Sierra Norte, providing varied terrain for cyclists. A portion of the municipality is within the Sierra Norte Natural Park, characterized by rolling hills, dense forests of holm oaks and cork oaks, and several reservoirs like José Torán and Retortillo. This mix of natural features creates a dynamic environment for mountain bike routes. Best mountain bike trails around La Puebla De Los Infantes The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra Morena Trail, a 15.9 miles (25.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain through the Sierra Morena foothills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Embalse de José Toran a Mirador de las Palomas - circular, an easy 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. This circular route offers scenic views around the José Torán Reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Colada del Campillo Loop, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail leading through forested areas and open countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Mountain biking around La Puebla De Los Infantes is defined by its mix of rolling hills, dense forests, and reservoir-side paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly routes to more challenging moderate trails. The routes in La Puebla De Los Infantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 90 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Puebla De Los Infantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: There are nearly 20 mountain bike trails around La Puebla De Los Infantes, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: Yes, La Puebla De Los Infantes offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992176337/embalse-de-jose-toran-a-mirador-de-las-palomas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de José Toran a Mirador de las Palomas - circular&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.3-mile (13.3 km) route that provides scenic views around the José Torán Reservoir.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: The terrain in La Puebla De Los Infantes is quite diverse, transitioning from the fertile plains of the Guadalquivir Valley to the foothills of the Sierra Norte. You can expect a mix of rolling hills, dense forests of holm oaks and cork oaks, and paths alongside reservoirs. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992132548/sierra-morena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail&lt;/a&gt;, feature varied terrain through the Sierra Morena foothills.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular mountain bike routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992176337/embalse-de-jose-toran-a-mirador-de-las-palomas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de José Toran a Mirador de las Palomas - circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994332034/colada-del-campillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colada del Campillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride through forested areas and open countryside. For longer rides, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Old Town – San Calixto Fountain loop from Palma del Río&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19379548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Las Palomas – José Torán Reservoir loop from Camping Puebla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer views of the region's natural beauty. You can ride alongside the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;José Torán Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3756949" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Retortillo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which are prominent features. The area also includes parts of the Sierra Norte Natural Park, known for its diverse flora and fauna. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bembézar Dam&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3415384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor Reservoir - Guadalquivir River&lt;/a&gt; on some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: The best time for mountain biking in La Puebla De Los Infantes is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential winter rains.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: While most routes are moderate, experienced riders can find longer, more demanding trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19379548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Las Palomas – José Torán Reservoir loop from Camping Puebla&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route spanning over 48 km with significant elevation gain, offering a good challenge.

Q: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for longer rides, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, a repair kit, and a fully charged phone. Sun protection is also recommended, especially during sunnier months.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails here?
A: The komoot community highly rates the mountain biking in La Puebla De Los Infantes, with an average of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene reservoir-side paths to challenging forest trails, and the overall natural beauty of the Sierra Norte foothills.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific cafes directly on the trails are limited, La Puebla De Los Infantes itself offers options for food and refreshments. Planning your route to pass through or near the town can allow for a stop to refuel.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes start from or pass through areas where roadside parking or small designated parking spots can be found, particularly near popular points of interest like the José Torán Reservoir or within La Puebla De Los Infantes itself. It's recommended to check the specific route details on komoot for suggested starting points.
tips: Good slope to climb and reach the viewpoint. Beautiful views of the reservoir inaugurated in 1991, located in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The reservoir is named in honor of the civil engineer José Torán Peláez, who died in 1981. The dam is located on the Guadalbarcar river.

The Mirador de Las Palomas is a spectacular place located in La Puebla de los Infantes, in the province of Seville. From the viewpoint you can see the reservoir, which is an ideal place for fishing and bird watching.

The José Torán Reservoir is located in the bed of the Arroyo Guadalbarcar/Víbora river, forming part of the municipalities of Constantina, Lora del Río and Puebla de los Infantes, in the province of Seville in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park.
The dam is easily accessible from Lora del Río, shortly after passing the Castle of Setefilla, the reservoir is located about 12 km from this town.

waypoints.name: Ascent to Mirador de Las Palomas
